mainEntity.name: How is optimizing for Google AI Overviews different from regular SEO, and can I really influence which pages get cited?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You can’t force Google AI Overviews to show your business, but you can heavily influence it by optimizing the same signals that drive organic search and featured snippets. AI Overviews are essentially the next layer on top of strong SEO.

Traditional SEO focuses on keywords, backlinks, and on-page optimization. AI Overview optimization adds emphasis on question-based content, schema markup, and E‑E‑A‑T. The underlying ranking signals are similar; what changes is how the answers are synthesized.

Key factors:
- Solid organic rankings or visibility for target queries
- Clear Q&A structures and FAQ sections
- Appropriate schema markup and clean HTML
- Strong reputation and topical authority

Practically, integrate AI Overview optimization into your SEO roadmap: for each primary topic, build a pillar page plus related subpages, all with structured Q&A and schema. Monitor which pages start appearing in AI Overviews and iterate.

Soft positioning: Think of AI Overview optimization as “SEO plus answer design,” not as a separate marketing channel.
acceptedAnswer.description: Optimizing for AI Overviews extends normal SEO: you still need rankings and authority, but you also design clear question‑and‑answer content, add schema, and strengthen E‑E‑A‑T. You can’t force inclusion, yet these signals make your pages far more likely to be cited.
